column18:
Optimum Learning for All StudentsImplementing Alberta’s 2018 Professional Practice StandardsA Longitudinal, Mixed Methods Research Study ONLINE SURVEY FOR TEACHERS On behalf of the Optimum Learning for All Students Research Team, thank you for your interest in participating in the online survey. It will take approximately 30 minutes to complete this survey. You are encouraged to take time to consider and provide a complete answer for every question. If you prefer to complete the survey in more than one sitting, you can exit at any point and resume at the same place by clicking the link. The final deadline for completing the survey is November 17th, 2021. Please note that the link you have been provided with can only be used by one person. If you forward it to others they will not be able to use it to complete the survey. Purpose of the StudyAlberta Education has authorized this 4-year longitudinal mixed methods research study designed to assess, deepen, and extend the implementation process for Alberta’s three professional practice standards: The Teaching Quality Standard, the Leadership Quality Standard, and the Superintendent Leadership Quality Standard. We are interested in your thoughts about and experience with the professional practice standards. The study will provide (a) independent, objective insights into the effectiveness of provincial and school authority implementation strategies and (b) recommendations on how best to support implementation in various Alberta contexts.   Informed ConsentParticipation in this study is voluntary and all information collected, used and/or disclosed will be used for research purposes only and administered as per the requirements of Alberta’s Freedom of Information and Protection of Privacy Act (FOIP Act).This consent section is only part of the process of informed consent. If you want more details about something mentioned here, or information not included here, you should feel free to ask. Please take the time to read this carefully.The University of Calgary Conjoint Faculties Research Ethics Board has approved this research study. What Will I Be Asked to Do?As a participant in this study you will be asked to complete this survey.Your participation in this study or lack thereof, will have no consequences on your employment with your school jurisdiction or relationship with the University of Calgary. What type of Personal Information Will Be Collected?Should you agree to participate in this survey, no personal information will be retained. Are there Risks or Benefits if I Participate?There are no foreseeable risks or benefits related to participation in this survey, other than the professional learning opportunities inherent in the research design. Participation in this study is completely voluntary. What Happens to the Information I Provide?Your survey responses cannot be removed once the survey has been submitted. The findings will be shared with the larger educational community through presentations, peer reviewed journals, or in book format. A final research report will be provided to the funder, the Alberta Ministry of Education. It must be understood that the data and findings remain the property of the researchers. Anonymous survey data will be retained indefinitely for possible future research purposes.  Questions/ConcernsIf you have any further questions or need clarification regarding this research and/or your participation, please contact the Principal Investigator Dr. Sharon Friesen from the Werklund School of Education, University of Calgary at (403)220-5625 or email sfriesen@ucalgary.ca.  If you have any concerns about the way you’ve been treated as a participant, please contact an Ethics Resource Officer, Research Services Office, University of Calgary at (403)220-6289 / (403)220-8640; email cfreb@ucalgary.ca.
By marking "I agree", you are consenting to participate in the survey.1.      I have read the above information and agree to participate in the survey.
